Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Markun Zusman Compton Llp To Comply With Pltf'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set No One
Off Calendar Apr-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David Wakukawa, Esq. of Markun Zusman Freniere Compton LLP, Tel. 415.438.4515, for defendant and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF BRAD SANDERS' Motion For Order Compelling Markun Zusman Compton LLP To Comply With Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set No One is Denied, except as to production of legible documents. The Court finds that all responsive documents within the descriptive categories of Plaintiff's Demand for Production nos. 4, 5, and 6, created after September 22, 2011, would be protected from discovery as work product. Travelers Ins. Co. V. Superior Court (1983) 143 Cal.App.3d 436, 452. The Court finds that under the facts of this case, where the defendants state they produced all responsive documents before that date, no privilege log is required. To the extent any documents previously produced are illegible due to poor copy or scan quality, defendants shall produce legible copies (or images) within 14 days of this order. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge Pro Tem: Charels Geerhart; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
